feedback.text: I am someone that has been playing video games for almost 30 years and I have seen some games come and go due to licensing. I would like to say that the industry itself does well enough with the preservation of what they create, but unfortunately that is not the case. Video games used to be sold completely on a disc without the need to connect to some server for a day one patch or anything like that, but nowadays that isn't certain anymore. There is a game called Tony Hawk Pro Skater 5 which I bought for full price some years ago. But not the whole game was on the disc. Only the first level. The rest had to be downloaded through a server which the company has disabled, thus locking me out of most of my purchase. I never got a refund for it. I think it would benefit the Digital Fairness Act to start reading the EULAS for specifically video games and see how they are not forcable under European Law but also not any sort of fair.
